description_long: A cluster of small, daisy-like yellow-orange flowers is scattered across a patch of green foliage. The flowers have prominent, rounded petals radiating from a central disc, with some blooms appearing more isolated while others are grouped closely together. The petals are arranged in a radial pattern, and the flower heads vary slightly in size and orientation. Several green leaves, mostly ovate in shape, are visible beneath and among the flowers, adding texture and color contrast. The overall structure is low to the ground, with no tall stems elevating the flowers above the foliage. This arrangement is typical of ornamental ground-cover flowers often found in urban parks, roadside plantings, or landscaped gardens.
description: A dense cluster of yellow-orange daisy-like flowers with green ovate leaves, arranged close to the ground. The flowers are radially symmetrical and grouped in a natural, scattered pattern.
description_view_0: A top-down view showing the full spread of yellow-orange flowers and green leaves, with the clustered arrangement most visible.
description_view_1: An angled side view highlighting the layering of flowers and the scattered distribution, with more isolated blooms visible on the right.
description_view_2: A low side view emphasizing the horizontal spread and the close-to-ground nature of the flowers, with petals and leaves overlapping.
description_view_3: A rear-angled view showing the density of the central cluster and the extension of leaves and flowers outward.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, daisy-like configuration, with each flower displaying a single whorl of overlapping, rounded petals radiating symmetrically from a central disc.
